claim.content:
The paper asserts that the extended Brinkman–Rice picture with $U=\kappa\rho^{2}U_{c}$ better explains the Mott transition in the examined materials than the $d=\infty$ Hubbard model, supporting this claim by citing the qualitative difference in mechanisms (in the $d=\infty$ Hubbard model the coherent‑peak weight of the spectral function decreases with increasing $U$ and the Mott transition occurs via loss of coherence, whereas in the extended Brinkman–Rice picture the transition is associated with a diverging quasiparticle effective mass as $\rho\to1$) together with the fitted agreement of the extended Brinkman–Rice formula to selected specific‑heat data.
      X. Y. Zhang, M. J. Rozenberg, and G. Kotliar, Phys. Rev. Lett. 70, 1666 (1993)
